items.q: What is the difference between strength and power?
items.correct: Strength is maximum force; power is force times speed of movement.
items.random_wrong: Strength is for the upper body, power for the lower.
items.why: Students conflate strength and power. A strong person can lift heavy slowly; a powerful athlete generates force QUICKLY (sprinter, shot-putter). Power requires both force and velocity.

items.correct: Increases red blood cell production, improving oxygen-carrying capacity.
items.why: Students assume all PEDs work the same way. EPO is specifically for endurance - more red cells = more oxygen to working muscles. Anabolic steroids do something different (promote protein synthesis).

items.q: What type of joint is the knee?
items.why: Students confuse knee with hip. Both are in the leg, but the hip is ball-and-socket (multi-directional); the knee is a hinge (mostly flexion-extension). Ball-and-socket joints have more range.
The answer examiners want is: Optimal arousal is individual. “All athletes need low arousal” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
The answer examiners want is: Bodily symptoms (HR, sweating). “Worry and negative thoughts” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: If an athlete's IZOF spans state-anxiety scores 18–24 and their pre-game score is 30, deviation above zone is?
items.why: Exact answer: 6. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
